hypothesis: f_min 25 → 150 at composed #37 baseline. Preprocessing is the one axis not heavily probed at composed baseline. #13 (pre-#23) showed the 500-1000 Hz band is discriminative (removing it cost 0.077); #24 (pre-#23, f_min 25→100) regressed 0.06 on weaker k=3 architecture. At k=7, spatial context aggregation is better; raising f_min to 150 compresses mel-bin resolution onto [150, 1000] Hz (still contains 500-1000 Hz discriminative band) and drops 25-150 Hz bins (below S1/S2 fundamentals — testing whether harmonic structure alone suffices). Three outcomes: (a) metric > #37 → k=7 recovers the f_min raise via mid-high-band focus (preprocessing still has room to find a composed-baseline win); (b) regression like #24 → fundamental contributions matter enough that the mid-high focus can't recoup; (c) flat → filterbank redistribution is a noise lever. ls reverted 0.1→0.0 to isolate.
